#  Structures of Relaxor AFE

The macroscopic crystal structures were first examined by X-ray diffraction (XRD). As shown in Fig. 2a, all (Pb₀.₉₄₋ₓCdₓLa₀.₀₄)(Hf₀.₇Sn₀.₃)O₃ ceramics exhibit a pure perovskite structure without secondary phases, with diffraction peaks shifting to higher angles due to lattice contraction from Cd substitution. Rietveld refinement (Fig. 2b) confirms the Pbam space group characteristic of orthorhombic antiferroelectric order. Crucially, superlattice reflections associated with antiparallel Pb²⁺ displacement are observed in both XRD patterns (Fig. 2c, blue symbols) and selected-area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns (Fig. 2d), indicating that the long-range antiparallel ordering is not completely destroyed. However, the average modulation period N derived from SAED increases from 4.0 in PH to 4.5 in Cd3, signaling a progressive disruption of the regular antiparallel stacking.

The microscopic domain structures, visualized by dark-field TEM (Fig. 2e), reveal the breakdown of long-range order more directly. The PH sample displays highly regular 180° antiparallel domain boundaries, consistent with macroscopic AFE order. In Cd0, mild disorder appears alongside the antiparallel domains. Most strikingly, the Cd3 sample exhibits highly disordered heterogeneous dipolar structures (HDSs) with diffuse contrast, lacking sharp domain walls. This transition from ordered to disordered structures is further resolved at atomic scale by HAADF-STEM (Fig. 3). In PH (Fig. 3a), Pb²⁺ displacement vectors show remarkable regularity in both angle and magnitude, forming the classic “++--” pattern. In Cd0 (Fig. 3b), localized displacement deflections emerge. In Cd3 (Fig. 3c), the displacement sequence becomes a mixture of 3-2 and 2-2 stripes (N=4 and N=5), with highly disordered magnitude and direction distributions—the hallmark of HDSs. This progressive disordering, achieved through compositional substitution including a high-entropy approach, breaks down the macroscopic antiparallel polar order into local/short-range heterogeneous structures that are highly dynamic and responsive to external fields.
